more"}},"simpleText":"...more"},"showLessText":{"simpleText":"Show less"},"attributedDescriptionBodyText":{"content":"Pfizer, Moderna, AstraZeneca… will the vaccines from these laboratories provide sufficient overall immunity to stop the transmission of the coronavirus? Each of them boasts promising efficacy rates ranging from 70% to 95%. And in the absence of a treatment for the disease, the vaccination campaign that begins Sunday, December 27th in France represents significant hope.\n\nHowever, many factors remain to be considered to assess their impact in the coming months. Will their real-world effectiveness match that measured in clinical trials? Will they provide sufficiently long-lasting immunity? How many people will need to be vaccinated to curb the epidemic?\n\nThese uncertainties necessitate planning relevant vaccination campaigns to better determine who should be vaccinated first and at what pace. The hope is to one day return to “normal life.”\n\n==========\n\nSources:\n\nCalculation of herd immunity and control of the COVID-19 epidemic:\nhttp://covid-ete.ouvaton.org/Rapport2...\n\nVaccination rate needed based on the duration of immunity and vaccine efficacy:\nhttps://www.thelancet.com/journals/la...\n\nVaccination rate needed based on the distribution of ages and occupations in the population:\nhttps://science.sciencemag.org/conten...\n\nEvolution of the reproduction number R0 in France (page 28):\n\nhttps://www.santepubliquefrance.fr/ma...\n\nPlatform for sharing scientific Moderna, AstraZeneca… will the vaccines from these laboratories provide sufficient overall immunity to stop the transmission of the coronavirus? Each of them boasts promising efficacy rates ranging from 70% to 95%. And in the absence of a treatment for the disease, the vaccination campaign that begins Sunday, December 27th in France represents significant hope.\n\nHowever, many factors remain to be considered to assess their impact in the coming months. Will their real-world effectiveness match that measured in clinical trials? Will they provide sufficiently long-lasting immunity? How many people will need to be vaccinated to curb the epidemic?\n\nThese uncertainties necessitate planning relevant vaccination campaigns to better determine who should be vaccinated first and at what pace.
